classObjective: Graph the image of a polygon after a rotation of 90°, 180°, or 270° about a given point on the coordinate plane.
workedExamples: 13-3, Example 1 — Rotate Polygons About a Given Point Using Translate-Rotate-Translate Method
13-3, Example 2 — Rotate Polygons 90°, 180°, and 270° About the Origin
warmUp: What are the coordinate rules for a 90° rotation about the origin?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: translate-rotate-translate-back method for arbitrary center; 90°, 180°, 270° rotation rules; rotating polygons
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice rotating polygons about given points using coordinate rules
independentPractice: Practice problems graphing rotated figures including 90°, 180°, 270° rotations about various centers
assessment: Exit ticket: rotate the figure with vertices (1,1), (3,1), (3,4), (1,4) 90° clockwise about point (1,1)
capReflection: Why is it useful to have a method for rotating about any point, not just the origin?
